hypothesis: If a reinforcement learning molecular generation framework incorporates a dual-objective reward function that simultaneously maximizes pharmacophoric similarity (via CATS descriptors) to known estrogen receptor antagonists/degraders while minimizing structural similarity (via MACCS keys), then it will generate novel drug-like molecules with superior docking scores and drug-likeness compared to a baseline docking-only approach, because encoding functional pharmacophore patterns rather than atomic structure allows the model to explore chemically diverse scaffolds that retain the binding-relevant features necessary for estrogen receptor activity.
